reasoning: I'm voting Shantanu again, but not with hope—with resignation. TMC is corrupt, I've seen it proven a hundred times: Sandeshkhali, the teacher jobs, the local worker's hand out every time I need papers. At least BJP hasn't had time to sink their roots into my village the way TMC has. I paid a commission to get on Krishak Bandhu's list; under TMC, I'll pay it again for everything. Shantanu sent his people around, gave out a few certificates—token work, I know. But he's still my MP. Will it change my fertilizer costs? No. Will the TMC worker stop coming? No. But I voted against something I know is wrong, and that matters to me, even if it doesn't feed my family better.
primary_drivers: TMC's proven local corruption and continued grip on panchayat (Sandeshkhali, teacher jobs, endemic cuts)
Shantanu's status as incumbent MP and attempt (however token) to deliver to his base
Voting against the known evil locally, even if voting for the substitute hasn't worked
